ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Ni eba Hagar ṅõ, ni ená mũsũ; ši beni ena ake̱ ehĩe mũsũ le̱, ewura le̱ he guo̱ gbo ye̱ ehĩe.
translated: The mother of Hagar died, and her father; but she had a maternal aunt, to whom she went, and she brought her up.

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni beni Arpakšad fo̱ Šela se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-edfe̱ ke̱ etẽ, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.
translated: And Arphaxad begot Cainan, and Cainan begot Salah, and Salah begot Eber.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Ni Iokšon fe̱ Šeba ke̱ Dedan. Ni Dedan bii le̱ dši Ašurbii ke̱ Letušbii ke̱ Leumbii.
translated: They came from Sheba and Dedan; they traded with you in fine linens and cloth; their merchandise was turquoise, purple, and embroidered cloth.

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Ni Laban kē̱: Ehĩ ake̱ mike̱le̱ hãao fè noni mike̱le̱ aahã nū kroko; oke̱mi ahĩ ši!
translated: And Laban said: It is not right that I should give her to you without your serving me seven more years; what do you say?

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ni ená mũsũ ekoṅ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ekē̱: Edšakē̱ Iehowa enu ake̱ asumo̱o̱-mi, hewo̱ le̱ ehāmi bi ne̱ bũ, ni ewole̱ Simeon.
translated: And lo, a spirit took him, and lo, he spake, and he said: The Lord Jesus has taken my soul to-day, and I have seen and I have known, and Simeon.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ni ekē̱ ake̱: *Atšē̱ṅ ogbe̱i Iakob doṅṅ, ši Israel aatšē̱o; edšake̱ oke̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱ gbomei eši mple ni oye kũnim. *
translated: And it was: when Jacob had finished giving instructions to his sons, he gathered his feet together; then he lay on his bed, ready to join his ancestors.

ref: “Who is this Lord and why should I obey him?” the king replied. “I refuse to let you and your people go!”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ ake̱: Namo *dši Iehowa le̱ ni mabo egbē toi ní mahã Israel aya? Milee Iehowa, ni Israel hũ mihãã ame̱ya. *Dan.
translated: Then Pharaoh said: For who is the Lord, that I should obey His voice and let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and I will not let Israel go. - Dan.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Ni Laban here no̱ ni ekē̱ Iakob akē̱: Biyei nē̱ mibiyei ni, ni bii ne̱ hũ mibii ni, tōi ne̱ hũ mitōi ni, ni nibii ní onaä ne̱ fē le̱ minii ni; ši meni mawo mibiyei ne̱ ke̱ ame̱bii ni ame̱fo̱fo̱i le̱ afemo̱ ṅme̱ne̱?
translated: Here I am, and you are my uncle Laban's daughter, and I do not know whether it is good for me to stay with you or not; I have come with my livestock, my camels, my donkeys, and my flocks; I have come with my male and female servants; and now I am asking you, will you be good to me and allow me to stay with you, or should I go back to my master?

ref: But Jacob said, “I won't let my son Benjamin go down to Egypt with the rest of you. His brother is already dead, and he is the only son I have left. I am an old man, and if anything happens to him on the way, I'll die from sorrow, and all of you will be to blame.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Mibi le̱ ke̱nye eyaa; edšake̱ enyemi le̱ egbo, ni le̱ ekome eſe̱; ke̱dši ošrã ko yašrãle̱ ye̱ gbe̱ ni nyeyanyie le̱ no̱ le̱, no le̱ nyebaṅõ miwadši le̱ ke̱-ya gbohïĩadše ke̱ aw̌ere̱hō.
translated: The chiefs said: We do not know what he has done; his action is like a bird, we do not know where it will land; it flies and we do not know where it will alight, it is not clear what it intends to do, it is not clear what it will do.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Ši yino̱ ní dši edſe̱ le̱ no̱ le̱ ame̱akũ ame̱se̱ ke̱-aba bie̱ ekoṅ; edšake̱ Amorbii le̱ anõša le̱ náko ayi.
translated: The sun was setting, casting a warm orange glow over the landscape as the birds began to chirp and sing their evening songs.

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Ni Esau nye̱ Iakob ye̱ dšō̱mo̱ ni etše̱ dšō̱le̱ le̱ hewo̱. Ni Esau kē̱ ye̱ etšui mli ake̱: Gbĩĩ ní ake̱-bafó mitše̱ le̱ eše̱ etã, ni magbe minyemi Iakob.
translated: Esau was angry with Jacob because Esau had been cheated out of his blessing. He said to himself: The time has come to mourn for my father; then I will kill my brother Jacob.

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Ṅõ tšinayō ni eye afii etẽ, ke̱ tōabotia ní eye afii etẽ, ke̱ tõagbo ní eye afii etẽ, ke̱ kose̱-okpo ke̱ okpobi obahãmi.
translated: My head is aching, my body is tired, my mouth is dry, my stomach is growling, and I am hungry.

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Ni Rebeka kē̱ Isak ake̱: Midšeṅhĩle̱ edše mitšine ye̱ Hetbiyei le̱ ahewo̱. Ke̱-dši Iakob w̌e̱ Hetbiyei ni tame̱ mene̱mei ni dši šikpoṅ ne̱, no̱ biyei le̱ eko le̱, té mahĩ dšeṅ make̱ tẽṅ?
translated: And Rebekah said to Isaac: I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth: if Jacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these which are of the daughters of the land, what good shall my life be to me?

ref: Then the governor of Egypt told us, “I'll find out if you really are honest. Leave one of your brothers here with me, while you take the grain to your starving families.
text: Ni nū le̱ ni dši šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nuṅtšo̱ le̱ kē̱wo̱ akē̱: Ene̱ maṅõ-le ake̱ anokwafoi dšinye: nyešĩa nyenyemimei le̱ ateṅ mokome nyehãami, ni hõmo̱ ní yo̱ nyešĩai le̱ hewo̱ le̱ nyeṅõa niyenii ke̱-yaã,
translated: We are the ones who have been entrusted with the sacred tradition of our ancestors: it is not just a matter of speaking, it is a matter of doing; we must put our words into practice, otherwise our words are empty and without value.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Ni ame̱kē̱kē̱ ame̱be ake̱: Na, lamõi atše̱ le̱ baä.
translated: I'm going to tell you a story: Listen very well.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Ni Abram mīfã ke̱-mīya wuoyigbe̱ le̱ lolo.
translated: And Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Ni Iakob mli wo la, ni eke̱ Laban bé. Ni Iakob here no̱ ni ekē̱ Laban ake̱: Mẽ tõ mitõ, ni mẽ eša hũ mife, ni ohĩe edo̱ ke̱-nyie mise̱ ne̱ke̱ ne̱?
translated: And Jacob served Laban for seven years, but they seemed to him like a few days because of his love for her. And Jacob said to Laban: Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in to her.

ref: Later our father sent us back here to buy more grain.
text: Ni beni wo̱tše̱ kē̱ ake̱: Nyeyaä ekoṅ ni nyeyabea niyenii fiō ko nyebahãawo̱ le̱,
translated: My head is aching because I have a headache and my body is also aching.

ref: and he told the Egyptians: There are too many of those Israelites in our country, and they are becoming more powerful than we are.
text: Ni eke emaṅbii le̱ ake: Nyekwe̱a, Israelbii amaṅ le̱ yi efá ni ame̱ná hewale̱ hũ fèwo̱.
translated: The people are saying: Look, the Israelites are leaving and they are going to do to us what they did to the Egyptians.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Ni beni Abram nu ake̱ aṅõ enyemi le̱ nom le̱, eke̱ etšũdši ohai-etẽ ke̱ nyoṅma ke̱ kpãnyo̱ ni le tahenii ní afo̱ame̱ ye̱ ewe le̱ fã ta, ni etiuame̱ ke̱-te Dan.
translated: The Lord said to Abram, “Leave your country, your relatives, and your father’s household, and go to the land I will show you. I will make you a great nation, and I will bless you; I will make your name great, and you will be a blessing. I will bless those who bless you, and I will curse anyone who curses you; I will bless you and make you a blessing to all people, through you I will bless all nations, I will make you the father of many nations, as I made you the father of a multitude, and your name will be Abraham, the father of many nations, as the Lord spoke to him.
